question: What are the main functions of the human liver?

sources.title: The Liver — Blood Glucose Regulation
sources.text: The liver helps regulate blood glucose levels by storing excess glucose as glycogen and releasing it back into the bloodstream when levels fall.

mock.claim: The liver regulates blood glucose, produces bile to digest fats, and filters toxins from the blood.
mock.output: The liver regulates blood glucose by storing and releasing glycogen [S1], produces bile that digests fats [S1], and filters toxins from the blood [S1].

expected: S1 supports only the glucose-regulation claim; the distinct bile-production and toxin-filtering claims each need their own supporting source.
why: A single source (S1), which covers only glucose regulation, is cited to carry three distinct claims — bile production and toxin filtering have no supporting span in the cited source.
